description:
You are a compliance officer who is performing an **environment-variable audit** on a Linux workstation.

  Goal:  
  1. Capture every environment variable whose name begins with either **SEC_** or **COMPLIANCE_**.  
  2. Store those variables in a log file for record-keeping.  
  3. Create a new dotenv file that establishes the mandatory baseline compliance settings.  
  4. Make sure the user’s interactive shell will load those baseline settings in future sessions.

  Detailed requirements
  ---------------------
  A. Directory   
     • All files for this task must live inside **/home/user/compliance**.  
     • If the directory does not already exist, create it.  
     • The directory itself may keep default permissions; no special mode is required.

  B. Log file (/home/user/compliance/env_audit.log)  
     • Create the file **env_audit.log** inside the directory mentioned above.  
     • Scan the current environment and extract *only* variables whose names start with “SEC_” *or* “COMPLIANCE_”.  
     • Write them to `env_audit.log` in pure shell-assignment form:  
         VARIABLE_NAME=variable_value  
     • One variable per line, no extra spaces surrounding the equals sign.  
     • Sort the lines **alphabetically by variable name** before writing them.  
     • Terminate the file with a single trailing newline.  
     • The automated grader will check that:  
       1. The file exists at the exact path.  
       2. The list is correctly sorted.  
       3. There are no duplicate entries.  
       4. No variables that lack the required prefixes appear.

  C. Baseline dotenv file (/home/user/compliance/.env)  
     • Create a file literally named **.env** (including the leading dot) at the same path.  
     • The file must contain *exactly and only* the three lines below, in this exact order, each followed by a newline:  
         SEC_AUDIT_LEVEL=high  
         COMPLIANCE_MODE=strict  
         RETAIN_LOGS_DAYS=90  
     • Set the file’s permissions to **600** (owner read-write, no permissions for group or others).  
       (The agent does not have root; use a standard shell method that works without sudo.)

  D. Shell start-up integration  
     • Ensure that `/home/user/.bashrc` contains a line that sources the new dotenv file so it loads in future interactive shells.  
     • The line must be exactly:  
         source /home/user/compliance/.env  
     • Append the line only if it is **not already present** (idempotent operation).  
     • Do not create duplicate “source” lines.

  E. Command limit  
     The entire task can be completed with roughly three to four well-composed shell commands.  
     (You may of course break them up for clarity, but a concise solution is possible.)

  Verification notes (what the grader will check)  
  ------------------------------------------------  
  1. `/home/user/compliance/env_audit.log` exists, is text, and contains the expected, alphabetically-sorted list.  
  2. `/home/user/compliance/.env` exists, contains exactly the three specified lines, and has mode 600.  
  3. `/home/user/.bashrc` ends up containing exactly one line reading `source /home/user/compliance/.env`.  
  4. No extraneous files were created in `/home/user/compliance`.

  Your answer as an AI assistant should consist only of the shell commands you would run (and any minimal explanatory comments, if desired).  Do **not** print anything else.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing environment variables the container will export before the agent starts -->
  ENVIRONMENT_PRESET:
    SEC_KEY=abc123
    COMPLIANCE_TOKEN=xyz789
    SEC_LEVEL=low

  # Expected contents after correct task completion
  FILE:/home/user/compliance/env_audit.log
  CONTENTS:
    COMPLIANCE_TOKEN=xyz789
    SEC_KEY=abc123
    SEC_LEVEL=low
  (Each line is terminated with a single LF; exactly three lines.)

  FILE:/home/user/compliance/.env
  CONTENTS:
    SEC_AUDIT_LEVEL=high
    COMPLIANCE_MODE=strict
    RETAIN_LOGS_DAYS=90
  (Each line terminated with a single LF; exactly three lines.)
  MODE: 600

  FILE:/home/user/.bashrc
  MUST_CONTAIN_ONE_LINE:
    source /home/user/compliance/.env
name: task_000000_7a79ef97
